title: US CPI Forecast Snapshot - June 2026
summary: Oil collapse accelerates: Brent ~$72, WTI ~$69 (June 26) = deeper energy deflation for June CPI. 10Y yield faded to 4.41% (Jun 24) on growth fears. DXY 101.33. FOMC hawkish hold (dot plot +25bp) caps duration rally. Core likely +0.15% MoM with shelter normalizing; headline -0.15% on gasoline drag. June CPI = July 10 release.
baseCase: Below-consensus headline on oil collapse; core cooling but sticky services floor

release.name: CPI
release.thesis: Oil collapse (-25% in June) drives headline negative. Core softening as shelter normalizes but services ex-shelter sticky. FOMC hawkish hold limits market reaction.

scenarios.label: Base case
scenarios.outcome: Headline -0.15% m/m, Core +0.15% m/m - oil deflation dominates, core moderates
scenarios.marketReadthrough: 10Y yields drift lower (4.35-4.40%), DXY 100-101. Airlines (UAL) and chemicals (LYB) rally on jet fuel/naphtha margin expansion. Energy (XLE) under pressure. Tech (NVDA) limited upside due to FOMC hawkishness.
scenarios.invalidation: Iran deal collapses, WTI spikes above $80, or core services re-accelerate above +0.25%.

scenarios.label: Hotter inflation surprise
scenarios.outcome: Headline +0.05%+ m/m, Core +0.25%+ m/m - oil rebounds, services sticky
scenarios.marketReadthrough: 10Y yields spike (4.50%+), DXY 101.5+. Tech (NVDA) and REITs (AMT) selloff. USD long, energy (XLE) short-cover rally. Gold (GLD) squeeze.
scenarios.invalidation: Hot print driven by one-off energy component; core services actually stable.

scenarios.label: Cooler disinflation impulse
scenarios.outcome: Headline -0.25% m/m, Core +0.10% m/m - oil collapse fully captured, DXY compression
scenarios.marketReadthrough: 10Y yields drop (4.25-4.30%), DXY 99.5-100. Growth (QQQ) rallies, airlines (UAL) +10-15%. Homebuilders (DHI) on mortgage relief. Energy (XLE) -5%.
scenarios.invalidation: Market already priced a benign outcome; FOMC hawkish tone prevents rally.

setups.bias: Long duration on cooler or inline core
setups.trigger: Core CPI <= +0.15% and services ex-shelter stable; WTI stays below $72
setups.invalidation: Sticky core services above +0.25% with wages re-accelerating.

setups.bias: Cautious - FOMC hawkish limits multiple expansion
setups.trigger: 10Y yield drops below 4.35% after release and DXY softens below 100.5
setups.invalidation: Hot CPI with 10Y yield above 4.50% and DXY above 101.5.

setups.bias: Long - jet fuel collapse + summer demand
setups.trigger: WTI $68-72 and CPI headline soft; summer travel bookings strong
setups.invalidation: Iran deal collapses, WTI above $80, or travel demand disappoints.

setups.bias: Short - oil supply glut, Iran exports resuming, demand weak
setups.trigger: WTI below $72 and Hormuz traffic normalizes; API inventory weak demand
setups.invalidation: Iran deal collapses, Israel attacks, or OPEC+ cuts production.

setups.bias: Short - DXY 101 + FOMC hawkish + real yields rising
setups.trigger: DXY above 100.5 and 10Y yield above 4.40% on CPI day
setups.invalidation: DXY drops below 99.5 or geopolitical shock (Iran deal collapse).

watchItems.label: Fed path / Warsh
watchItems.note: FOMC hawkish hold on 16-17 Jun. PCE inflation revised to 3.6% for 2026. 3/18 members expect hike, 5/18 expect 50bp hikes.

watchItems.label: 10Y UST
watchItems.note: Key regime line. Below 4.35% = growth rally. Above 4.50% = risk-off. Oil pulling down, FOMC pushing up.

watchItems.label: WTI / Brent Oil
watchItems.note: June collapse -25%+. Brent 72.15 (26 Jun). Hormuz traffic normalizing. $70 line = headline CPI 0.0% risk.

watchItems.label: DXY
watchItems.note: Near 52-week high 101.80. FOMC hawkish + safe haven. Import deflation pressure on core goods.

keyDrivers: Oil collapse: WTI $69, Brent $72 (-25% in June) = gasoline CPI -8% to -10% m/m. Energy deflation is the dominant headline driver.
FOMC hawkish hold: Warsh's first meeting. Dot plot 3.75%-4.00% y/e 2026 = +25bp. PCE inflation revised sharply higher to 3.6% for 2026.
Shelter normalizing: Zillow/Apartment List slowing, BLS 6-12 month lag. OER back toward +0.20% MoM from April's +0.6% spike.
DXY 101.33: Strong dollar = import deflation on core goods. Partially offsets PPI passthrough.
Core services sticky: Wages +3.6% YoY, ISM services prices-paid elevated. Airline fares, recreation = upside risk.
Iran deal progress: Hormuz traffic normalizing, Persian Gulf exports at ~75% prewar levels. Supply glut risk = oil downside.
risks: Iran deal collapse: Nuclear inspectors denied, Israel/Lebanon tensions, US Congress opposition. WTI $80+ = CPI revision.
FOMC hawkish limits reaction: Market may 'sell the rally' even on soft CPI. Warsh 'one data point' rhetoric.
Demand weakness: API inventory weak demand = crude demand concerns. If global recession fears rise, oil and risk assets both fall.
Shelter lag: Faster deceleration than expected = core downside surprise. BLS methodology lags market rents by 6-12 months.
OPEC+ response: If oil prices stay below $70, Saudi Arabia may cut production to stabilize prices.
